title: lichen_l2_input: len=0 passed to lichen_link_rx may cause undefined behavior
description: In lichen_l2.c:lichen_l2_input() line 451, lichen_link_rx() is called with the raw 'len' parameter from the callback. If len=0 (empty packet), the behavior depends on lichen_link_rx() implementation. The lora_l2.c RX thread filters len==0 at line 141 before invoking the callback, but lichen_l2_input() is also a public API (declared in header). A direct caller could pass len=0, and the function does not validate len>0 before processing.
